llm_report.operational_summary: AHU is in cooling mode delivering ~14,410 cfm at 54.9°F, with mixed air 69.9°F from 36.8% outdoor air (65.9°F) and return air 71.5°F. Coil provides ~15°F drop (~68.5 kW_th), fan power ~4.85 kW.
llm_report.efficiency_assessment: Overall efficient (0 rule violations). Physics cross-check aligns: 1.08×cfm×ΔT ≈ 68.6 kW_th matches reported 68.5 kW_th; fan intensity ~0.34 W/cfm indicates low distribution energy, and OAT<RAT supports effective pre-cooling.
llm_report.key_issues: No issues detected. Potential optimization: with OAT below RAT, increasing OA (within humidity and minimum-ventilation limits) could further reduce coil load, though SAT 55°F still requires mechanical cooling.
llm_report.engineering_interpretation: Mixed-air calculation using 36.8% OA predicts ~69.4°F, within ~0.5°F of measured MAT, indicating coherent airflow/temperature measurements. Since OAT (65.9°F) > SAT (54.9°F), economizer alone cannot meet setpoint, but more OA would lower MAT toward 65.9°F and reduce coil ΔT by roughly a quarter if constraints allow.
llm_report.recommendation: Maintain current operation; continue to monitor economizer control to maximize pre-cooling during mild conditions while observing humidity and minimum-OA requirements. Re-verify OA fraction and temperature readings during tune-ups given current values are self-consistent.
evidence_used: Supply airflow 14,409.62 cfm; OA flow 5,297.5 cfm; OA fraction 0.368

llm_report.operational_summary: AHU is in cooling, delivering 13.9 kcfm at 55.1°F and removing ~64.9 kW sensible load (coil ΔT ~14.8°F from MAT 69.9°F). Outdoor air fraction is 39% (≈5.44 kcfm), and MAT aligns with mixing of 71.5°F return and 65.9°F outdoor air; fan draws ~4.6 kW.
llm_report.efficiency_assessment: Overall operation is efficient (0 rule violations). Physics cross-check confirms 1.08×CFM×ΔT ≈ 64.9 kW and fan intensity ~0.33 W/cfm, both indicative of good performance.
llm_report.key_issues: No faults flagged. Minor opportunity: OAT is below RAT; increased economizer use could trim coil load (roughly up to 17 kW) if humidity and building pressurization allow.
llm_report.engineering_interpretation: Mixed-air temperature sits correctly between OAT and RAT and matches the reported 39% OA fraction, supporting damper/sensor plausibility. With OAT ~65.9°F, even 100% OA would yield MAT ~66°F, so mechanical cooling is still needed to reach 55°F SAT; partial economizing is reasonable. Stable coil ΔT and SAT indicate controlled operation.
llm_report.recommendation: Maintain current control settings and monitor the economizer to opportunistically increase OA when outdoor conditions permit while maintaining humidity/pressure targets. Continue maintaining filtration and fan setup to sustain ~0.33 W/cfm performance.

llm_report.operational_summary: AHU is in cooling mode, delivering 55.0°F supply from 69.8°F mixed air at ~13,198 cfm. Outdoor air is ~28.8% while outdoor air (65.9°F) is cooler than return (71.5°F), and the coil is providing ~61.8 kW-thermal.
llm_report.efficiency_assessment: Needs review: cooler outdoor air was not leveraged to pre-cool mixed air, increasing mechanical cooling load. Fan power (3.9 kW) is modest; the primary inefficiency is economizer utilization.
llm_report.key_issues: Missed economizer opportunity: OAT (65.9°F) < MAT (69.8°F) < RAT (71.5°F) but OA fraction remained ~29%, keeping MAT elevated and increasing coil delta-T. Economizer logic or damper positioning did not ramp OA toward available free cooling.
llm_report.engineering_interpretation: Current sensible coil load ≈ 61.8 kW-thermal (consistent with 1.08×13,198 cfm×14.79°F and the reported 61.79). Raising OA toward 100% would pull MAT toward 65.9°F, reducing coil ΔT by ~3.96°F and saving ~16.5 kW-thermal (~27%); SAT 55°F would still need some mechanical cooling.
llm_report.recommendation: Review and tune economizer enable/high-limit settings and sequence so OA increases when OAT < RAT in cooling. Functionally test OA dampers (command to high OA and verify response) and, if capable, allow higher OA during similar conditions; trend OAT/MAT/SAT to confirm pre-cooling and reduced coil load.
evidence_used: Outdoor airflow 3,796 cfm of 13,198 cfm (OA fraction 0.288)
Calculated sensible load ≈ 61.8 kW-thermal; reported 61.79 kW-thermal
